comments: Tommy's intro and outro are missing from this recording
Tommy plays the wrong side of the Sweet Savage single

sessions.artist: Atomic Rooster Recorded 1981-10-16 for Friday Rock Show

sessions.details: first Tx 04 September 1981. Recorded: 1981-08-21. No known commercial release.

track_listing.details: B side of single - Take No Prisoners
V/A LP - The Friday Rock Show

track_listing.track: 'Surfer Joe and Moe the Sleaze' (LP - Re-ac-tor) Warner Brothers
'The Sign of the Southern Cross' (LP - Mob Rules) Vertigo

transcript.text: And that of course is Ozzy Osbourne and Blizzard of Oz. It's a track from the new Ozzy album called Diary of a Madman. It's out at the moment on Jet Records. The track you heard there was called Believer.
It's twelve and a half minutes after ten. I was in Hamburg last night. No, I'm not town dropping. I went over there to see an extremely good concert. Two people on the bill. Two acts on the bill.
Ozzy Osbourne, Blizzard of Oz and Saxon. And may I say that Ozzy, after his three weeks holiday in Barbados, or wherever he went, is now singing like a songbird.
and Saxon have really got to be one of the top ten bands in the world.
Now, last night in Hamburg, they were utterly faultless.
And before that, you heard Rainbow from The Best of Rainbow.
It's a new double album out on Polydor.
The track was called Long Live Rock and Roll.
The album features loads of remixes of many famous tracks
that they've put together over the years,
including Stargazer and Kill the King, A Light in the Black,
Man on the Silver Mountain, All Night Long,
I Surrender, Starstruck, and others.
Good double album, it really is.
quick mention here for a gentleman by the name of duncan mclaren who lives in london wanted me to
say especially today hello to sonia mcmahon from cretan and the message to sonia is he hopes that
this message is going to make up in part for missing the priest gig at leicester
also he wants me to say hello to everybody at bad lake school in coventry
quick mention to chris who's got a 22nd birthday today according to claire pemberton the six-foot
art student who lives in adamsdale in cardiff this 22nd birthday of yours chris is the oncoming
of gray hair and walking sticks ah the reformed atomic rooster well without a doubt honesty is
the best policy i have to tell you i played the wrong side of that record i should have played
the a side which is called take no prisoners that's the b side of the latest single by sweet
savage it's out on their own label called park records the track i played there was called
killing time my apologies the sweet savage over there in belfast and also to tony wilson who's
probably jumping up and down in the medieval studios now play the a side next week it's
22 and a half minutes after 10.
Before that, you heard the first of four from Atomic Rooster,
who are made up these days of Vincent Crane,
John Ducan, Paul Hammond, and Spig out of Huangcheng.
He's playing the bass.
The track was Death Walks Behind You.
And here are the Starfighters.
Humble opinion, that's a really superb piece of music.
It came out way back in 1978 on the album put out by Styx
called Pieces of Eight.
The track was called Renegade.
And, of course, they're in this country at the moment.
And I played that piece of music for a lady by the name of Marge.
from Philip Heems, who lives in Stoke-on-Trent in Staffordshire.
So play that bit of music called Renegade by Styx.
Little Headbanger Marge, just to prove that I love her,
that she's absolutely crazy about this record.
Also say hello to some of our friends,
Terry and Sue and Chez, I think it is,
Sparky and Steve and Kipper,
Vince and Animal, and say a special hello to Buffalo Bill,
and anybody else that knows Phil.
Lovely little card here that has gone on the front of it
contains Danish pig's kidney, and it comes from Plymouth.
The advanced type person, it says.
I've noticed a distinct lack of requests on your show from down here.
It's from Mr. Jem in Plymouth.
Says I've definitely got to say hello to Pinky, Paul and Melanie,
Martin, Chopper and his cat, Joanna and Paula somewhere near Derby,
Sue, Maggie and Christine in Brighouse,
Kathy, Nick down the road, Boneman, Henry Pig, Raggy Alcohaffi,
and generally everybody else who's heard of Mr. Jem down in Plymouth.
P.S. If you don't pay this request, Chopper will hang his cat.
So on your own head be it.
Oh, don't do that, Chopper.
Now, there's lots of albums, of course, coming out at this time of the year for the Christmas market,
but there's one which is very, very special and close to our hearts.
It's called The Friday Rock Show, and on that you'll find this track.
That you'll find is a single, I think it's on the Polydor label,
it's called Play It Again, it's by the recently reformed Atomic Rooster,
but that particular version is a session track that we recorded in the BBC Maida Vale studios quite recently.
Its title is Play It Again.
And before that, something from an album that you should find in the shops toward the latter part of next week.
It's an album called The Friday Rock Show, featuring music from, well, this here programme.
All the tracks on the album are rather like Metal Explosion, if you like.
They're all part of sessions that have been done specially for The Friday Rock Show.
And the acts that you will find on the album are Diamond Head, whom you heard doing Don't You Ever Leave,
also Sweet Savage, which find Last Flight, Black Axe, Demon, Spider and Zero.
It's out on BBC Records, and of course we need the money.
So buy one for your granny and turn her on to really good music.
It's 18 minutes to 11.
It's from the new album by the Tigers of Pantang called Crazy Nights.
It's out on the MCA record label.
The track you hear was called Never Satisfied.
The second one by Starfighters.
Tomorrow, a peak session by them.
That was entitled Devil's Driving.
Just about 10 to 11 on the Friday Rock Show.
The Friday Night Connection.
The answer to last week's.
But first, the tracks that we play.
jimmy hendrix tax-free from war heroes then from thank christ for the bomb the groundhogs and
soldier and for the yes album yes doing starship trooper comparatively simple one really starship
trooper soldier war heroes the 10 pound record token is going to go to a gentleman who's attending
a college at the moment it's called the college lord mayor's walk york y037ex that's craig morrison
Craig, congratulations.
We'll be sending you the £10 record token just as soon as we can.
And as always, part of the deal is that you have to put together your own Friday night connection.
Three bits of music that have a theme or connection contained therein.
And incidentally, this week we've got four runners-up.
And I'll be telling you who those people are, and they'll be getting a free book
following these three bits that were put together by Mr. Morrison.
Connection put together by Craig Morrison, the College Lord Mayor's Walk in York.
Three pieces of music that have a very definite connection.
And because I had to, well, didn't have to, I was happy to shoot over to Germany for last night's concert in Hamburg, where I saw Ozzy Osbourne and Blizzard of Oz and Saxon.
I had no idea what this week's Friday Night Connection was at all.
Tony Wilson did choose it, right, out of the piles and piles of letters.
And Mr. Morrison, you're going to know exactly what I'm talking about here, because you put it together.
But while those three bits of music were playing, I purposefully didn't look at the answer, which, of course, is contained in Craig's letter.
And I couldn't guess for the life of me what the connection was
And I then looked
And now I've discovered
Thank you Tony Wilson very much indeed
I've really got to give the answer to that one on the air next week
How I'm going to do that with decorum
I have no comprehension
Good one Craig
Or should I say good six
Or even more
If you think you know the answer to this week's Friday Night Connection
Jot it down on a postcard
Any form of communication you care to employ
And send it through
The Friday Night Connection
The Friday Rock Show
BBC Radio 1
London W1A4WW
Don't forget to put together your own connection as well please
Three bits of music that's, well, interconnected
By the way, our birthday program is coming up soon
Our third birthday program
What I'd like you to do, if you will
Is to let us know what your three all-time favourite rock tracks are
If you do that, I'd be very grateful
If you're going to write in and do the connection
Please put that information on a separate bit of paper.
It helps us.
Your three all-time favorite rock tracks.
And send those to the same address, right?
Buddy Rock Show, BBC Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
We've been given free some books that we're now going to give to certain people free.
The book is called HM A to Z,
and it's described on the front cover as being the definitive encyclopedia of heavy metal
from ACDC through Led Zeppelin to ZZ Top.
Full history of each band, line-up discography, photos by Brian Harrigan.
Four copies to give away and they're going to go to, I think it's Yao or Tal Brooks at number 5 Chartwell Avenue, Ruddington in Nottingham.
Also to Monohor Raja of 33 Oakfield Road, West Croydon, Surrey.
And Sally Westwater of 4 Greencroft, Riverdale Park, Redcar in Cleveland.

is en route.
